commit.message:
Add personal best tracking with confetti animation

Backend:
- /api/submit now returns is_new_pb (bool) and prev_best, computed by
  combining COUNT+MAX into a single pre-insert query (no schema change)

Frontend:
- PB badge on each unlocked scene card (⭐ PB: 87%) using best_scores
  from userProgress, updates on every renderCards() call
- #pbBanner inside score panel — shows "⭐ New Personal Best!" when
  is_new_pb is true, gold animated pill with scale-in keyframe
- showPBBlast() — 70-piece CSS confetti burst + large gold text overlay,
  auto-removes after 3.2s; fires alongside the score panel banner
- Personal Bests section in My Progress modal: scenes sorted by best
  score descending, shows ↑ arrow when latest attempt beats previous one

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add friend challenge system

Backend:
- challenges table (challenge_id, challenger_username, scene_id, score_to_beat)
- POST /api/challenge — creates a challenge link, returns short hex ID + URL
- GET /api/challenge/{id} — returns challenge details (public, no auth)
- GET /challenge/{id} — SPA route: serves index.html so JS handles routing

Frontend:
- URL routing in boot: detects /challenge/[id] path, loads challenge screen
- #challengeScreen — full-page challenge card showing challenger, score, movie
- "Accept Challenge" button opens scene modal with challengeCtx set
- After scoring: showChallengeResult() shows YOU WON / So close overlay
- "⚔ Challenge a Friend" button in score panel → POST to create challenge
- Share panel: copy link + WhatsApp share button
- Login/register from challenge page flows back into challenge after auth

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add XP/leveling system and achievements (#14)

* Add XP service: award_xp, level formula, coins, unlock_achievement

- New services/xp.rs with level_for_xp (floor(sqrt(xp/50))+1),
  coins_for_xp (xp/10), award_xp, unlock_achievement (idempotent),
  check_and_unlock_achievements, and unlock_champion helpers
- Wire award_xp + check_and_unlock_achievements into record_play (5 XP)
  and submit_score (10 XP); award 100 XP on game upload
- Unlock champion achievement when user achieves rank #1 on leaderboard
- 10 unit tests covering level boundaries, coins formula, award_xp, and
  achievement idempotency

Update napari to 0.7.0a3 to fix vispy arcball crash

napari 0.7.0a3 requires vispy>=0.16.1 which includes the fix for the arcball
camera ValueError (too many values to unpack when shift+dragging in 3D view).
napari 0.6.6 pinned vispy<0.16 which excluded the fix.

Verified: no breaking API changes affect our code. We explicitly set
camera.angles=(0,0,180) so the 0.7.0 default angle change is irrelevant.

commit.message:
Fix NSS DB symlink and CA cert import for RA containers

- Replace conf/alias directory (created by pki-server create) with symlink
  to alias/ where certs are actually imported
- Import root CA and intermediate CA certs individually with separate
  nicknames so pki nss-cert-export can build the certificate chain
- Without this, pki-server run fails with "Certificate chain not found"

commit.message:
Fix: remove static collection-card-image from block_order

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

iter32e: diversity-aware early-stop + 3600s hard cap (CPU Qwen constraint)

1. scripts/run_once.py: _brief_probe_filtered now returns distinct_sources.
   Early-stop condition requires probe_distinct >= 3 OR last tier before
   stopping on probe_kept >= target. Prevents TechCrunch-only tier-1 from
   stopping prematurely (distinct=2) → triggers SOURCE_DIVERSITY_WARN → DBE.
   BRIEF_HYDRATE_BATCH + BRIEF_FAST_EARLY_STOP logs include probe_distinct.

2. scripts/verify_online.ps1: default budget 1800s → 3600s (hard cap).
   Root cause: CPU Qwen at ~1 tok/sec; primary card_build (9 items × 3 roles ×
   40s) = ~1440s + Z0 hydration = ~1500s total. If DBE triggers (1831s
   additional), total = ~3284s which exceeds 1800s but fits 3600s.
   Soft-warn at 1800s preserved in footer and WARN log.
   Effective budget change propagated to run_once.py via PIPELINE_TIME_BUDGET_SEC env.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf: parallelize CI into setup/typecheck/test/bundle-report jobs (#475)

Split the single sequential CI job into 4 parallel jobs:
- setup: install + build shared + cache workspace
- typecheck: runs in parallel with test
- test: unit tests without coverage (drops ~15s overhead per file)
- bundle-report: runs in parallel, non-blocking
- ci: gate job that preserves branch protection check name

Also use --reporter=dot for less console I/O during tests.

Expected improvement: ~4.5min → ~2min.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

trigger CI on all branch pushes, not just main

Triggering on all pushes lets us validate and plan on feature
branches before merging. Apply remains gated to main only.

Remove the "pull_request" trigger and PR comment posting since
we rely on push events instead.

fix: use pg.Pool with ssl rejectUnauthorized:false for Vercel

Supabase's TLS cert isn't trusted by Node.js's default CA bundle in
serverless environments. Explicitly set ssl.rejectUnauthorized = false
on the pg Pool so connections succeed on Vercel.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Phase 4 Backend - Add Hysteria2 protocol support

Add Hysteria2 QUIC-based VPN protocol support to go-node-manager backend.
Hysteria2 is optimized for high-latency/lossy networks (UDP 443).

Changes:
- Added HYSTERIA2 to ProtocolType enum in models/protocol.go
- Added Hysteria2Config struct for API responses
- Updated SingBoxConfig to include Hysteria2 inbound support
  - buildHysteria2Inbound() creates QUIC/TLS inbound configuration
  - Supports password-based client authentication
  - Optional obfuscation parameter for future extension
- Updated buildClientConfig() to return appropriate config type per protocol
- Added ProtocolConfig.Obfs field for Hysteria2 obfuscation settings
- Updated configuration system to support Hysteria2 settings:
  - Enabled flag
  - Port (default 443 for UDP)
  - Obfuscation setting (defaults to "none")
- Registered Hysteria2 manager in main.go initialization

API Contract:
- POST /api/v1/clients/{clientId}/hysteria2/connect
  - Returns: Hysteria2Config JSON with credentials
- All sing-box protocols now supported:
  - WIREGUARD (UDP 51820)
  - SHADOWSOCKS (TCP 8443)
  - VLESS (TCP 8880)
  - HYSTERIA2 (UDP 443) <- NEW

Architecture:
- libbox already supports Hysteria2 protocol via sing-box
- Backend just needs to generate configs and manage users
- Mobile clients (iOS/Android) receive config and pass to libbox
- libbox handles QUIC tunneling and protocol negotiation

No breaking changes - fully backward compatible with existing protocols.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: NEXT.md — bots go silent is the top blocking issue

Captain experienced 10+ minute silence from all bots. Root cause: idle
containers don't process queued messages. Idle-wake fix deployed on
Poseidon but HERACLES container needs restart. Also noted: bots lack
any autonomous work mechanism — they are purely reactive.

commit.message:
Zálohovací systém: 14 oprav odolnosti (validace, rollback, lock, WAL)

- ZIP validace: CRC integrity check (testzip) před safety backup
- Rollback: při selhání restore automatická obnova ze safety backup
- File lock: souběžné restore operace blokované přes .restore_lock
- Disk space check: 2× heuristika před vytvořením zálohy
- Auto-cleanup: max 10 záloh, nejstarší se automaticky mažou
- .env backup: přidán do ZIP a obnoven při restore
- manifest.json: metadata (timestamp, verze) v každé záloze
- Chunked copy: shutil.copyfileobj místo read/write celého souboru
- WAL mode: PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL při každém DB připojení
- Post-restore migrace: vrací list warnings pro UI feedback
- Error handling: try/except ve všech restore endpointech
- UI: nové chybové hlášky (neplatny, selhani, probihajici)
- Celková velikost záloh zobrazena pod tabulkou
- Odstraněn nebezpečný endpoint obnovit-adresar

commit.message:
Add perry setup wizard, pre-flight validation, and friendly distribution error messages

- Add `perry setup [android|ios|macos]` interactive credential wizard
  - Android: keystore generation/selection + Google Play service account setup
  - iOS: App Store Connect API key, .p12 certificate, provisioning profile (3 steps)
  - macOS: API key + Mac distribution certificate with App Store/Developer ID choice
  - Validates credentials (JSON fields, PEM format, file extensions) before saving
  - Saves to ~/.perry/config.toml, prints perry.toml snippet at end

- Add pre-flight validation in `perry publish` (validate_credentials_for_distribute)
  - Fails immediately before tarball creation if credentials are missing
  - Android + distribute = "playstore" requires google play key → run perry setup android
  - iOS/macOS + distribute = "appstore"/"testflight" requires API creds → perry setup ios/macos
  - Validates "playstore:X" track names (internal/alpha/beta/production only)

- Fatal Play Store upload failures in pipeline.rs (was non-fatal warning)
- parse_playstore_track(): "playstore" → internal, "playstore:beta" → beta, etc.
- is_playstore now matches "playstore:X" variants
- iOS/macOS pipelines now return Err when distribute is set but API creds are missing

- map_playstore_error(): 401/403/404/429/5xx → actionable messages with URLs
- translate_altool_error(): maps 9 ITMS codes + patterns to friendly guidance
- Fixed UTF-8 panic: truncate error body at char boundary (not byte boundary)
- Fixed "401" false positive in altool error detection (require "401 Unauthorized" etc.)
- Make PerryConfig/AppleSavedConfig/IosSavedConfig/AndroidSavedConfig pub(crate)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: run continuous aggregate DDL outside transaction block

TimescaleDB CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW ... WITH (timescaledb.continuous)
cannot run inside a transaction. Commit the Alembic transaction before
creating continuous aggregates. Enable transaction_per_migration in env.py
so subsequent migrations get their own fresh transaction.
